name: Blue-Ribbon Chili (Dottie's Winner)
recipeIngredient: 5 1/2 pounds trimmed beef brisket, cut into 3/4-inch cubes
4 jalapenos, finely chopped (or more if you prefer extra hot)
3 tablespoons pure red mild chile powder (such as dark New Mexico)
6 cups beef stock or canned broth or water
42 ounces canned Italian peeled tomatoes, coarsely chopped with their liquid
2 scallions, white and tender green portions, thinly sliced (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the cumin seeds in a small dry skillet over moderate heat, stirring constantly, until fragrant, about 2 minutes. Grind the cumin in a spice mill or mortar.
2. Heat a large enameled cast-iron casserole. Season the brisket with salt and pepper. Working in batches, add the meat to the casserole and cook over moderately high heat until well-browned all over, about 8 minutes. Transfer each batch to a large plate.
3. Add the garlic, jalapenos, and onions to the casserole and cook over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 4 minutes. Add the commercial chili powder and pure red chile powder, coriander, and half of the ground cumin and cook, stirring, for 2 minutes.
4. Return the cooked brisket to the casserole and add the beef stock, beer, tomatoes and their liquid, and oregano. Bring to a boil over moderately high heat, then lower the heat and simmer gently, stirring occasionally, for 3 hours.
5. Stir in the ground chuck, season with salt, and cook until the brisket is very tender and the sauce is thickened, about 1 hour longer. Stir in the remaining cumin and simmer for 15 minutes. Garnish with the scallions and serve.
